item.title: Managed Threat Hunting Meets the Challenge of the Tenacious Adversary
item.description: Learn the tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) stealthy adversaries employ that go beyond what can be observed or detected. They even troubleshoot when their activities are prevented, but enterprise-wide visibility and monitoring by experienced analysts can disrupt these dedicated bad actors.

item.title: CrowdStrike Provides Free Dashboard to Identify Vulnerable Macs
item.description: CrowdStrike has addressed a new macOS vulnerability, CVE-2018-4407, and is supporting its Mac users by releasing a dashboard that helps Falcon platform customers identify vulnerable Macs in their environments.

item.title: Managed Threat Hunting Bridges the Talent Gap
item.description: In an article from The Security Ledger, CrowdStrike’s Scott Taschler explains how managed threat hunting can bridge the IT security talent gap that is jeopardizing cybersecurity and burdening organizations worldwide.

item.title: CrowdStrike Receives Highest Overall Score in the 2018 Gartner Peer Insights Customers’ Choice for EPP
item.description: CrowdStrike has been named in the 2018 Gartner Peer Insights Customers’ Choice for Endpoint Protection Platforms. This follows other Gartner recognition for CrowdStrike of highest and furthest "Visionary" in the Gartner MQ for EPP and top 3 in all categories for the Gartner Critical Capabilities for EPP report.

item.title: CrowdStrike Demystifies Threats with MITRE ATT&CK
item.description: CrowdStrike leverages the MITRE ATT&CK framework to add context to security workflows and reduce time to triage and investigate threats.

item.title: Helix Kitten | Threat Actor Profile | CrowdStrike
item.description: HELIX KITTEN is an Iran-based threat actor targeting the aerospace, energy, financial, government, hospitality, and telecommunications business verticals.

item.title: Farewell to Kelihos and ZOMBIE SPIDER
item.description: Learn about Kelihos malware and its operator from CrowdStrike Falcon® Intel experts providing technical analysis, attribution, and context around the threat actor and an outlook section with a YARA rule for detection.

item.title: MITRE ATT&CK: Why Detections and Tainted Telemetry are Required for an Effective EDR Solution
item.description: In the recent MITRE ATT&CK Evaluations, MITRE used some terms that appear to have been misunderstood, even by some test participants. Here we'll look at the terms as defined by MITRE and dig into some relevant details.

item.title: Big Data, Graph, and the Cloud: Three Keys to Stopping Today’s Threats
item.description: Graph database technology is emerging as a key tool in the cybersecurity arsenal, helping organizations put big data to work to detect advanced threats and stop breaches. Learn more about CrowdStrike Threat Graph, the industry’s largest and most sophisticated graph database.

item.title: Confessions of a Responder: The Hardest Part of Incident Response Investigations
item.description: Get expert guidance on incident response from an IR investigator. Learn the importance of deploying a technology to scope the incident, some common deployment challenges and what organizations can do to be better prepared if and when an incident occurs.

item.title: Adversary Extends Persistence by Modifying System Binaries
item.description: The CrowdStrike Falcon® OverWatch team has identified new tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) being employed by actors engaged in “living off the land” – using native system utilities to collect information, move laterally, and extend their foothold in an environment.

item.title: Digging into BokBot’s Core Module
item.description: This technical blog digs into BokBot's core module and shows how BokBot infections have become more prevalent due to Emotet campaigns that leverage BokBot as a means to go after a victim’s personal banking information.

item.title: Visibility and Granular Control: The Secret to Securing USB Devices In the Workplace
item.description: Adversaries use USB devices to invade organization networks because they are not only broadly popular and social engineering tactics such as the USB drop work. Learn how CrowdStrike Falcon® Device Control gives you the visibility and speed you need to enable safe USB device use and stop attacks.

item.title: Analyzing Targeted Intrusions Through the ATT&CK Framework Lens [VIDEO]
item.description: This video highlights cases of unique adversary tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) observed by CrowdStrike security analysts, comparing different methods used by cyber adversary groups and how they have evolved over time.

item.title: Widespread DNS Hijacking Activity Targets Multiple Sectors
item.description: CrowdStrike Falcon® Intelligence has been researching reports of widespread DNS hijacking targeting organizations in multiple sectors including government, technology, insurance, and civilian aviation, as well as ISPs and infrastructure providers.

item.title: Uncovering The “Unknown Unknowns”: Why Threat Hunting is a Security Must-Have
item.description: Threat hunting allows organizations to detect and respond to advanced threats by leveraging knowledge of the threat landscape along with the proper tools and data to find sophisticated adversaries.

item.title: CrowdStrike Expands Its Horizons in India with New Innovation and Development Center in Pune
item.description: CrowdStrike opens a new Innovation and Development Center in Pune, India, as part of its global expansion and focus on enhancing the Falcon platform.

item.title: Goblin Panda Adversary | Threat Actor Profile | CrowdStrike
item.description: GOBLIN PANDA is a China-based threat actor targeting the aerospace & defense, energy, food & tobacco, government, marine services, and technology sectors.

item.title: Two Birds, One STONE PANDA
item.description: CrowdStrike Falcon® Intelligence has observed threat actors with ties to historic STONE PANDA (APT10) engaged in major Chinese intrusion campaigns and associated with other adversaries such as GOTHIC PANDA,

item.title: Podcast Features George Kurtz on What it Takes to Build a Unicorn
item.description: CrowdStrike CEO and co-founder George Kurtz offers his insight and guidance on what it takes to build a unicorn including what it took for CrowdStrike to reach its $3B valuation.

item.title: Leading Global Brands Secure Themselves with CrowdStrike
item.description: Learn why global brands are choosing CrowdStrike to secure their endpoints and why Telstra, Australia's largest Telco, is both a customer and an investor as published in The Australian.

item.title: Is There Such a Thing as a Malicious PowerShell Command?
item.description: Learn how to better understand PowerShell and why this powerful and flexible scripting language is used to help administrators be more efficient, while also serving as a malicious tool for bad actors.

item.title: Evaluating Your Organization's Security Approach: Tips For BOD and C-Level Execs
item.description: Find out the questions Boards of Directors and C-level executives should ask to better understand their organizations’ cybersecurity strategies and processes, so they can play a more active role.

item.title: Should You Worry About Software Supply Chain Attacks?
item.description: Read a survey report on software supply chain attacks, which occur when an injection of malicious code is made to the source of a signed and trusted application — learn why they are on the rise and what it takes to better prepare your organization.

item.title: CrowdStrike Named a Leader in the IDC Marketscape: U.S. Incident Readiness, Response and Resiliency Services 2018 Vendor Assessment.
item.description: CrowdStrike is named a leader in the IDC Marketscape: U.S. Incident Readiness, Response and Resiliency Services 2018 Vendor Assessment. Learn why the IDC MarketScape positioned CrowdStrike as a leader.

item.title: Leveraging Falcon Sandbox to Detect and Analyze Malicious PDFs Containing Zero-Day Exploits
item.description: PDF samples containing exploits for zero-day vulnerabilities have been found. Learn how CrowdStrike Falcon® Sandbox detects, analyzes and stops them before they can impact your organization.

item.title: Your Jenkins Belongs to Us Now: Abusing Continuous Integration Systems
item.description: Learn how cyber adversaries leverage vulnerabilities to control Jenkins open source automation servers used by development teams, and how you can protect your organization from these attacks.

item.title: Cutwail Spam Campaign Uses Steganography to Distribute URLZone
item.description: CrowdStrike analyzed a new Cutwail spam campaign from NARWHAL SPIDER that uses digital steganography to distribute URLZone.

item.title: DUNGEON SPIDER | Threat Actor Profile | CrowdStrike
item.description: DUNGEON SPIDER is a group of eCrime hackers that conduct opportunistic targeting aimed at deploying the Locky ransomware. Learn about their targets and methods.

item.title: OverWatch Insights: Reviewing a New Intrusion Targeting Mac Systems
item.description: While Mac enterprise networks are not as common as Windows, and subject to less targeting by adversaries, recent CrowdStrike® Falcon OverWatch™ observations shed light on sophisticated tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) targeting Mac environments.
